concepts.title: equations reducible to quadratic equation by substitution
concepts.conceptBody:
Higher Degree Equations

The equation \( p(x) = a_{0} x^{n} + a_{1} x^{n-1} + \ldots + a_{n-1} x + a_{n} = 0 \) …(i)

Where the coefficients \( a_{0}, a_{1}, \ldots, a_{n} \in \mathbb{R} \) (or \(\mathbb{C}\) ) and \( a_{0} \ne 0 \) is called an equation of n-th degree, which has exactly n roots \( \alpha_{1}, \alpha_{2}, \ldots, \alpha_{n} \in \mathbb{C} \), then we can write

\( p(x) = a_{0} (x - \alpha_{1}) (x - \alpha_{2}) \ldots (x - \alpha_{n}) \)

\( = a_{0} \left\{ x^{n} - (\Sigma \alpha_{1}) x^{n-1} + (\Sigma \alpha_{1} \alpha_{2}) x^{n-2} - \ldots + (-1)^{n} \alpha_{1} \alpha_{2} \ldots \alpha_{n} \right\} \) …(ii)

Comparing (i) and (ii), \( \Sigma \alpha_{1} = \alpha_{1} + \alpha_{2} + \ldots + \alpha_{n} = -\frac{a_{1}}{a_{0}} \)

\( \Sigma \alpha_{1} \alpha_{2} = \alpha_{1} \alpha_{2} + \ldots + \alpha_{n-1} \alpha_{n} = \frac{a_{2}}{a_{0}} \)

and so on and \( \alpha_{1} \alpha_{2} \ldots \alpha_{n} = (-1)^{n} \frac{a_{n}}{a_{0}} \)

Cubic equation: When \( n = 3 \), the equation is a cubic of the form \( ax^{3} + bx^{2} + cx + d = 0 \), and we have in this case

\( \alpha + \beta + \gamma = -\frac{b}{a}; \quad \alpha \beta + \beta \gamma + \gamma \alpha = \frac{c}{a}; \quad \alpha \beta \gamma = -\frac{d}{a} \)

Biquadratic equation: If \( \alpha, \beta, \gamma, \delta \) are roots of the biquadratic equation \( ax^{4} + bx^{3} + cx^{2} + dx + e = 0 \), then

\( \sigma_{1} = \alpha + \beta + \gamma + \delta = -\frac{b}{a} \)

\( \sigma_{2} = \alpha \beta + \alpha \gamma + \alpha \delta + \beta \gamma + \beta \delta + \gamma \delta = \frac{c}{a} \)

\( \sigma_{3} = \alpha \beta \gamma + \alpha \beta \delta + \alpha \gamma \delta + \beta \gamma \delta = -\frac{d}{a} \)

\( \sigma_{4} = \alpha \beta \gamma \delta = \frac{e}{a} \)

Formation of a polynomial equation from given roots: If \( \alpha_{1}, \alpha_{2}, \alpha_{3}, \ldots, \alpha_{n} \) are the roots of a polynomial equation of degree n, then the equation is

\( x^{n} - \sigma_{1} x^{n-1} + \sigma_{2} x^{n-2} - \sigma_{3} x^{n-3} + \ldots + (-1)^{n} \sigma_{n} = 0 \)

where \( \sigma_{r} = \sum \alpha_{1} \alpha_{2} \ldots \alpha_{r} \).

Cubic equation: If \( \alpha, \beta, \gamma \) are the roots of a cubic equation, then the equation is \( x^{3} - \sigma_{1} x^{2} + \sigma_{2} x - \sigma_{3} = 0 \) or \( x^{3} - (\alpha + \beta + \gamma) x^{2} + (\alpha \beta + \alpha \gamma + \beta \gamma) x - \alpha \beta \gamma = 0 \)

Biquadratic Equation: If \( \alpha, \beta, \gamma, \delta \) are the roots of a biquadratic equation, then the equation is

\( x^{4} - \sigma_{1} x^{3} + \sigma_{2} x^{2} - \sigma_{3} x + \sigma_{4} = 0 \)

or \( x^{4} - (\alpha + \beta + \gamma + \delta) x^{3} + (\alpha \beta + \alpha \gamma + \alpha \delta + \beta \gamma + \beta \delta + \gamma \delta) x^{2} - (\alpha \beta \gamma + \alpha \beta \delta + \alpha \gamma \delta + \beta \gamma \delta) x + \alpha \beta \gamma \delta = 0 \)

What is the number of solutions to the equation $x^n-5 x^{n-1}+6 x^{n-2}=0$ ?
Consider the equation $x^n-5 x^{n-1}+6 x^{n-2}=0$, where $n$ is a positive integer. What is the sum of all possible real roots of this equation?
What is the sum of the roots of the equation $x^3-6 x^2+11 x-6=0$ ?
